result:
**[UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT]{.underline}**

**[WESTERN DISTRICT OF TEXAS]{.underline}**

**[AUSTIN DIVISION]{.underline}**

**STANDING ORDER FOR CIVIL CASES** **BEFORE THE HONORABLE CATHERINE M. ALVAREZ** **UNITED STATES DISTRICT JUDGE**

*Effective Date: September 1, 2023* *Supersedes all prior standing orders for civil cases*

**[I. GENERAL PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**1.1 Scope and Applicability**

This Standing Order governs all civil cases assigned to the Honorable Catherine M. Alvarez in the Austin Division of the United States District Court for the Western District of Texas. Unless otherwise ordered, this Standing Order supplements the Local Rules of the Western District of Texas and the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure. In the event of any conflict between this Standing Order and the Local Rules, this Standing Order shall control. Nothing in this Standing Order shall be construed to modify or supersede the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, 28 U.S.C., or any applicable statute.

**1.2 Familiarity Required**

All counsel and pro se litigants appearing before this Court shall be familiar with and comply with this Standing Order, the Local Rules of the Western District of Texas, and the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure. Failure to comply with this Standing Order may result in the striking of filings, denial of relief, imposition of sanctions, or other appropriate measures at the Court\'s discretion.

**1.3 Communication with Chambers**

No party or counsel shall communicate with the Court\'s chambers regarding any substantive matter except by formal filing on the docket or at a scheduled hearing or conference. Scheduling matters, requests for extensions of time, and other administrative inquiries shall be directed to the Courtroom Deputy Clerk at the telephone number and email address listed on the Court\'s webpage. All communications with chambers must include all parties or their counsel; ex parte communications are strictly prohibited except as permitted by law.

**1.4 Citations and Authorities**

All legal memoranda shall cite to official reporters where available. Citations to unreported or unpublished opinions must include a notation that the opinion is unpublished. Counsel shall indicate whether any cited authority is binding or persuasive in this jurisdiction. String citations are disfavored; counsel should cite only the most pertinent authorities supporting each proposition. Parenthetical explanations following citations are strongly encouraged.

**1.5 Pro Hac Vice Admissions**

Any attorney seeking admission pro hac vice shall comply with Local Rule AT-1 and shall file a motion for admission accompanied by the requisite fee. Local counsel must be designated and must accept responsibility for familiarity with and compliance with local rules and this Standing Order.

**[II. FORMATTING REQUIREMENTS FOR ALL FILINGS]{.underline}**

**2.1 General Format**

All filings, including motions, briefs, memoranda, responses, and replies, shall comply with the following formatting requirements unless otherwise specified herein:

> \(a\) **Font:** 12-point Times New Roman, Garamond, or Century Schoolbook. Footnotes may be in 10-point font of the same typeface.
>
> \(b\) **Spacing:** Double-spaced text in the body. Block quotations of 50 words or more shall be single-spaced and indented on both sides. Footnotes shall be single-spaced.
>
> \(c\) **Margins:** One (1) inch on all sides (top, bottom, left, and right).
>
> \(d\) **Page Numbering:** All pages shall be numbered consecutively at the bottom center of each page, beginning with page 1 on the first page of the filing (exclusive of the cover page, if any).
>
> \(e\) **Line Numbering:** Line numbering is not required and should not be used.
>
> \(f\) **Paper Size:** Letter size (8.5 x 11 inches).

**2.2 Caption Requirements**

All filings shall bear a caption conforming to the following format. The caption shall appear on the first page of every filing and shall include:

> \(a\) The name of the court (United States District Court, Western District of Texas, Austin Division);
>
> \(b\) The full case number, including the judge\'s initials (e.g., 1:23-cv-00847-CMA);
>
> \(c\) The names of all parties, with the party\'s designation (Plaintiff or Defendant) indicated. In cases with numerous parties, the caption may identify the first-named Plaintiff and the first-named Defendant followed by \"et al.\" only after all parties have been identified in the initial pleading;
>
> \(d\) The title of the document (e.g., \"Defendant\'s Motion to Dismiss Pursuant to Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 12(b)(6)\"); and
>
> \(e\) If the filing is a motion, the response deadline computed under this Standing Order or the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

**2.3 Signature Blocks**

All filings shall include a signature block for counsel of record. The signature block shall contain:

> \(a\) The handwritten or electronic signature of counsel (an \"/s/\" signature is acceptable for CM/ECF filings);
>
> \(b\) Counsel\'s printed name;
>
> \(c\) Counsel\'s State Bar number;
>
> \(d\) The name of counsel\'s law firm;
>
> \(e\) The firm\'s mailing address, telephone number, facsimile number (if applicable), and email address; and
>
> \(f\) An identification of the party represented.

**2.4 Certificate of Service**

Every filing shall include a certificate of service certifying that the filing has been served on all counsel of record and unrepresented parties in compliance with Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 5 and Local Rule CV-5. Service by CM/ECF constitutes service on all registered CM/ECF users. For any party not registered for CM/ECF, the certificate shall state the method of service (e.g., first-class mail, email, or hand delivery) and the date and address of service.

**[III. MOTIONS PRACTICE]{.underline}**

**3.1 Certificate of Conference**

Before filing any non-dispositive motion, the movant shall confer in good faith with opposing counsel in an effort to resolve or narrow the disputed issues. The motion shall include a certificate of conference signed by counsel, certifying one of the following:

> \(a\) That counsel for the movant personally conferred (by telephone or in person, not solely by email or letter) with counsel for the non-movant, specifying the date and manner of the conference, and that the parties were unable to reach agreement on the relief sought;
>
> \(b\) That counsel for the movant made reasonable, good-faith efforts to confer with counsel for the non-movant, specifying the dates and methods of attempted contact, and that opposing counsel failed to respond or was unavailable; or
>
> \(c\) That the nature of the motion is such that conferral is not practicable (e.g., a motion involving a legal question on which no compromise is possible). In such cases, counsel shall state the specific reasons conferral was not practicable.

**Dispositive motions, including motions to dismiss and motions for summary judgment, are also subject to the certificate of conference requirement.** Counsel for the movant shall make a good-faith effort to confer with opposing counsel to determine whether any claims or defenses may be voluntarily narrowed or dismissed prior to filing. The certificate of conference shall be filed as a separate document concurrently with the motion and shall detail the conferral efforts made and the results thereof. Failure to file the required certificate of conference may result in the motion being stricken or its consideration being deferred.

**3.2 Page Limits**

The following page limits apply to all motions and briefs filed in civil cases before this Court:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Filing**                                      **Page Limit**
  ----------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------
  Motions to Dismiss (Rule 12(b) and 12(c))       25 pages

  Motions for Summary Judgment                    30 pages

  Responses to Dispositive Motions                Same as the motion to which the response is directed

  Reply Briefs (all dispositive motions)          15 pages

  Non-Dispositive Motions                         15 pages

  Responses to Non-Dispositive Motions            15 pages

  Replies on Non-Dispositive Motions              8 pages

  Daubert / Motions to Exclude Expert Testimony   20 pages

  Motions in Limine                               15 pages (collective; not per motion)
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

These page limits are exclusive of the caption page, table of contents, table of authorities, signature block, certificate of service, and certificate of conference, but inclusive of all substantive argument, statement of facts, and statement of issues. Appendices and exhibits attached to the brief do not count against the page limit, but argumentative content shall not be placed in appendices to circumvent these limits.

Requests to exceed these page limits shall be made by motion filed no later than seven (7) days before the filing deadline. The motion must state the specific reasons why the page limit is insufficient and the number of additional pages requested. Motions for leave to exceed page limits are disfavored and will be granted only upon a showing of good cause.

**3.3 Briefing Schedule for Motions to Dismiss and Dispositive Motions**

Unless otherwise ordered by the Court, the following briefing schedule shall apply:

> \(a\) **Response.** The non-movant\'s response shall be filed within twenty-one (21) days after the filing of the motion.
>
> \(b\) **Reply.** The movant\'s reply, if any, shall be filed within fourteen (14) days after the filing of the response.
>
> \(c\) **Sur-Reply.** Sur-replies are not permitted except by leave of Court. A motion for leave to file a sur-reply must be filed within seven (7) days after the filing of the reply and must attach the proposed sur-reply as an exhibit. Leave will be granted only where the reply raises new arguments or authorities not fairly presented in the opening motion.

**3.4 Post-Answer Motions to Dismiss**

Consistent with the practice of this Court and the Western District of Texas, a party that has filed an Answer may file a motion to dismiss under Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 12(b)(6) --- and the Court shall treat such motion as a motion to dismiss, not as a motion for judgment on the pleadings under Rule 12(c) --- provided that the following conditions are met:

> \(a\) The movant preserved the Rule 12(b)(6) defense in its Answer, either by asserting the defense affirmatively or by specifically reserving the right to file a motion to dismiss;
>
> \(b\) The motion is filed within the time permitted for dispositive motions as established by the Court\'s scheduling order or case management order;
>
> \(c\) No party will be prejudiced by the Court\'s consideration of the motion as a motion to dismiss rather than as a motion for judgment on the pleadings; and
>
> \(d\) The movant identifies in its motion the specific grounds preserved in the Answer that form the basis for the motion to dismiss.

When a post-answer motion to dismiss is filed under this provision, the Court shall apply the same standard applicable to a pre-answer motion under Rule 12(b)(6): the Court will accept all well-pleaded factual allegations in the complaint as true, draw all reasonable inferences in favor of the non-movant, and determine whether the complaint states a claim to relief that is plausible on its face. *See Bell Atl. Corp. v. Twombly*, 550 U.S. 544, 570 (2007); *Ashcroft v. Iqbal*, 556 U.S. 662, 678 (2009). The Court will not consider matters outside the pleadings unless the motion is converted to a motion for summary judgment pursuant to Rule 12(d), in which case the Court shall provide all parties reasonable opportunity to present pertinent materials.

Documents that may be considered on a Rule 12(b)(6) motion without conversion to summary judgment include: (i) documents attached to the complaint, (ii) documents attached to the motion to dismiss that are referenced in the complaint and central to the plaintiff\'s claims, and (iii) matters of which the Court may take judicial notice under Federal Rule of Evidence 201.

**3.5 Proposed Orders**

All motions shall be accompanied by a proposed order granting the relief sought. Proposed orders shall be submitted in Microsoft Word (.docx) format as a separate filing on the docket. Proposed orders shall not be attached to or embedded within the motion itself. The proposed order shall:

> \(a\) Recite the full caption of the case;
>
> \(b\) Identify the motion to which the proposed order relates by title and docket entry number (if known at the time of filing; if not, counsel shall update the proposed order with the correct docket entry number within one business day of filing the motion);
>
> \(c\) Set forth the specific relief requested, with sufficient detail that the order is self-executing;
>
> \(d\) Include a signature line for the presiding judge; and
>
> \(e\) Not include argumentative recitals or findings of fact unless the Court has specifically requested proposed findings.

**3.6 Oral Argument**

Oral argument on motions is at the discretion of the Court. Parties should not assume that oral argument will be held. If a party believes oral argument would materially assist the Court\'s resolution of a motion, the party may include a request for oral argument in the conclusion of its brief. Such requests shall briefly state the reasons oral argument is warranted. The Court will notify the parties if oral argument is scheduled.

**[IV. PLEADINGS]{.underline}**

**4.1 Amended Pleadings**

Motions for leave to amend pleadings shall comply with Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 15 and Local Rule CV-15. A motion for leave to amend shall attach the proposed amended pleading as an exhibit and shall include a redline comparison showing all additions, deletions, and other changes from the prior pleading.

**4.2 Heightened Pleading Standards**

The Court will rigorously enforce the pleading requirements of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure. Claims sounding in fraud or mistake must comply with the particularity requirements of Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 9(b). Specifically, the complaint must identify the \"who, what, when, where, and how\" of the alleged fraud --- the identity of the person making the misrepresentation, the content of the misrepresentation, the time and place of the misrepresentation, and the method by which the misrepresentation was communicated. General averments of fraud, reliance on \"information and belief\" allegations without a stated factual basis, and group pleading that fails to attribute specific statements to specific individuals will not satisfy Rule 9(b). *See Benchmark Elecs., Inc. v. J.M. Huber Corp.*, 343 F.3d 719, 724 (5th Cir. 2003).

**4.3 Pleading Damages**

Parties asserting claims for damages shall plead their damages with reasonable specificity. Conclusory allegations of damages or speculative projections unsupported by factual allegations will be scrutinized on a motion to dismiss. Where a plaintiff seeks consequential, special, or punitive damages, the complaint shall allege facts showing the legal basis for such damages and the causal connection between the defendant\'s conduct and the claimed harm.

**[V. DISCOVERY]{.underline}**

**5.1 Discovery Limits**

Unless otherwise ordered, the discovery limits set forth in the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure shall apply. Each side is limited to 25 interrogatories (including discrete sub-parts), 30 requests for production, and 30 requests for admission. Depositions shall not exceed 10 per side, and no deposition shall exceed 7 hours of testimony in a single day.

**5.2 Discovery Disputes**

Before filing any discovery motion, counsel must confer in good faith to attempt to resolve the dispute without judicial intervention. The Court strongly encourages the resolution of discovery disputes through the meet-and-confer process. If a motion to compel or motion for protective order is necessary, the motion shall include a detailed certificate of conference describing the dates, participants, and substance of all conferral efforts. Discovery motions that do not include the required certificate of conference will be summarily denied.

**5.3 Electronically Stored Information**

The parties shall meet and confer regarding the preservation, collection, review, and production of electronically stored information (\"ESI\") no later than the date of the Rule 26(f) conference. The parties are encouraged to adopt a stipulated ESI protocol. Disputes regarding ESI shall be presented to the Court promptly.

**[VI. CASE MANAGEMENT]{.underline}**

**6.1 Initial Case Management Conference**

The Court will conduct an initial Case Management Conference in all civil cases, typically within 60 days after the filing of the last responsive pleading or the entry of a scheduling order, whichever is earlier. Counsel shall meet and confer prior to the Case Management Conference and shall submit a Joint Case Management Report using the form available on the Court\'s webpage no later than five (5) business days before the conference.

**6.2 Scheduling Orders**

The Court will enter a Scheduling Order following the initial Case Management Conference. The Scheduling Order will establish deadlines for, among other things, joinder of parties and amendment of pleadings, completion of fact discovery, disclosure of expert witnesses, completion of expert discovery, filing of dispositive motions, and filing of pretrial materials. Extensions of Scheduling Order deadlines are disfavored and will be granted only upon a showing of good cause under Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 16(b)(4).

**6.3 Settlement Conferences**

The Court is available to conduct settlement conferences upon the joint request of the parties. The Court may also order the parties to participate in mediation or other alternative dispute resolution proceedings. Requests for settlement conferences should be made through the Courtroom Deputy Clerk.

**[VII. TRIAL PROCEDURES]{.underline}**

**7.1 Pretrial Submissions**

No later than thirty (30) days before the trial date, each party shall file the following pretrial materials: (a) proposed jury instructions and verdict forms (in jury cases); (b) exhibit lists, with objections to opposing party\'s exhibits noted; (c) witness lists, with estimated time for each witness; and (d) motions in limine.

**7.2 Trial Briefs**

Each party shall file a trial brief no later than fourteen (14) days before the trial date. The trial brief shall not exceed 25 pages and shall address the key legal issues to be tried, the elements of each claim or defense, and any anticipated evidentiary issues.

**7.3 Courtroom Decorum**

Counsel and parties shall conduct themselves professionally at all times. Counsel shall stand when addressing the Court or examining witnesses. Personal attacks on opposing counsel are prohibited and may result in sanctions. Electronic devices must be silenced. Spectators and media shall comply with the Court\'s general orders regarding courtroom access and recording.

**[VIII. MISCELLANEOUS]{.underline}**

**8.1 Courtesy Copies**

The Court does not require courtesy copies of filings unless specifically requested. If the Court requests a courtesy copy, it shall be delivered to chambers within one (1) business day of filing and shall be marked \"COURTESY COPY\" on the first page.

**8.2 Sealed Filings**

Motions to seal documents shall comply with Local Rule CV-5(a) and must articulate specific facts establishing that the documents meet the legal standard for sealing. Blanket designations of documents as confidential, reliance solely on a protective order, or conclusory assertions of competitive harm are insufficient to justify sealing. The Court will apply the standard set forth in *Nixon v. Warner Commc\'ns, Inc.*, 435 U.S. 589 (1978), and its progeny.

**8.3 Withdrawal of Counsel**

Motions to withdraw as counsel shall comply with Local Rule AT-7 and must include a statement that the client has been notified of the withdrawal and of all pending deadlines and scheduled hearings.

**8.4 Amendments to This Standing Order**

This Standing Order may be amended at any time by order of the Court. Amended versions will be posted on the Court\'s webpage and filed on the docket in all pending civil cases assigned to this Court.

**IT IS SO ORDERED.**

Signed on this 1st day of September, 2023, in Austin, Texas.

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

THE HONORABLE CATHERINE M. ALVAREZ UNITED STATES DISTRICT JUDGE WESTERN DISTRICT OF TEXAS AUSTIN DIVISION

Distribution: This Standing Order is available on the Court\'s webpage at www.txwd.uscourts.gov and shall be served upon all counsel of record in civil cases assigned to Judge Alvarez. Counsel are responsible for monitoring the Court\'s webpage for any amendments or updates to this Standing Order.

**WHEREAS**, Licensee is a regional healthcare IT services provider operating electronic health record integration and analytics platforms for hospitals and clinics across Texas and Louisiana;

**WHEREAS**, Licensee desires to license from Licensor, and Licensor desires to license to Licensee, certain software modules of the NexusCore platform, specifically the Healthcare Analytics Module, to replace Licensee\'s existing legacy analytics platform known as \"HealthBridge 4.0\";

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements herein contained, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

**[ARTICLE I --- DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 1.1 Definitions.** As used in this Agreement, the following capitalized terms shall have the meanings set forth below. \"**Agreement**\" has the meaning set forth in the preamble.

\"**Applicable Law**\" means all applicable federal, state, local, and foreign laws, statutes, regulations, ordinances, rules, orders, judgments, decrees, and governmental requirements, including, without limitation, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, as amended (\"HIPAA\"), the Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act (\"HITECH\"), and all implementing regulations thereunder.

\"**Business Day**\" means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday, or a day on which commercial banks in Austin, Texas are authorized or required by Applicable Law to be closed.

\"**Change Order**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 5.4.

\"**Confidential Information**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 7.4.

\"**Control**\" (including the terms \"**Controlled by**\" and \"**under common Control with**\") means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise. For the avoidance of doubt, a Person shall be deemed to Control another Person if such first Person owns, directly or indirectly, more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting securities of such other Person.

\"**Data Migration Advisory Services**\" means the advisory consulting services described in Exhibit C, pursuant to which Licensor shall provide guidance and recommendations regarding the migration of Licensee\'s legacy data into the Licensed Software. \"**Deliverables**\" means all work product, documentation, configurations, customizations, reports, templates, and other materials developed or delivered by Licensor to Licensee in the course of performing the Implementation Services under any Statement of Work.

\"**Documentation**\" means the user guides, technical manuals, online help files, release notes, specifications, and other documentation provided or made available by Licensor to Licensee with respect to the Licensed Software, as updated by Licensor from time to time.

\"**Effective Date**\" has the meaning set forth in the preamble.

\"**Go-Live Date**\" means the date on which Go-Live is achieved. The target Go-Live Date for Statement of Work #1 is September 1, 2022 (the \"**Target Go-Live Date**\"), subject to adjustment in accordance with Section 3.5.

\"**HIPAA**\" means the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, as amended, and all implementing regulations thereunder.

\"**Implementation Services**\" means the implementation, configuration, customization, integration, training, and related professional services to be provided by Licensor as described in the applicable Statement of Work.

\"**Initial Term**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 11.1.

\"**Intellectual Property Rights**\" means all intellectual property rights, including patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, service marks, trade names, domain names, copyrights, copyright registrations, trade secrets, know-how, inventions (whether or not patentable), algorithms, software (in source code and object code form), databases, data compilations, designs, and all other intellectual property and proprietary rights recognized under the laws of any jurisdiction worldwide.

\"**License**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 2.1.

\"**License Fees**\" means the fees payable by Licensee to Licensor for the License, as set forth in Exhibit D.

\"**License Term**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 11.1.

\"**Licensed Software**\" means the NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module, including all modules, components, features, and functionalities described in Exhibit A, as updated, modified, or enhanced by Licensor from time to time during the License Term.

\"**Licensee**\" has the meaning set forth in the preamble.

\"**Licensee Data**\" means all data, information, content, and materials submitted, uploaded, transmitted, or otherwise provided by or on behalf of Licensee or its Authorized Users to the Licensed Software or to Licensor in connection with this Agreement, including all patient data, clinical data, operational data, financial data, and analytics data.

\"**Licensor**\" has the meaning set forth in the preamble.

\"**Losses**\" means all losses, liabilities, damages, costs, expenses, penalties, fines, and judgments (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and court costs) incurred by a Party.

\"**Maintenance and Support Services**\" means the maintenance and technical support services to be provided by Licensor during the License Term, as described in Article IV and the Service Level Agreement attached as Exhibit B.

\"**Material Nonconformity**\" means a failure of the Licensed Software to perform substantially in accordance with the Documentation that materially impairs Licensee\'s use of the Licensed Software for its intended purposes.

\"**NexusCore**\" means Licensor\'s proprietary cloud-based enterprise resource planning and data analytics software platform.

\"**Party**\" and \"**Parties**\" have the meanings set forth in the preamble.

\"**Person**\" means an individual, corporation, partnership, limited liability company, trust, association, joint venture, governmental authority, or other entity.

\"**Personal Data**\" means any data that constitutes \"protected health information\" as defined under HIPAA or \"personal data\" or \"personally identifiable information\" as defined under any applicable data protection or privacy law.

\"**Professional Services Fees**\" means the fees payable by Licensee to Licensor for the Implementation Services, Training Services, and Data Migration Advisory Services, as set forth in Exhibit D.

\"**Renewal Term**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 11.1.

\"**Schedule**\" means a schedule attached to and incorporated into this Agreement.

\"**Service Level Agreement**\" or \"**SLA**\" means the service level agreement attached as Exhibit B, which sets forth the performance standards, uptime commitments, response times, and escalation procedures applicable to the Licensed Software and the Maintenance and Support Services.

\"**Statement of Work**\" or \"**SOW**\" means a statement of work executed by the Parties and attached to or incorporated into this Agreement, which sets forth the scope, timeline, deliverables, milestones, responsibilities, and fees for specific Implementation Services.

\"**Training Services**\" means the end-user and administrator training services to be provided by Licensor as described in the applicable Statement of Work.

**Section 1.2 Rules of Construction.** Unless the context otherwise requires: (a) words in the singular include the plural, and words in the plural include the singular; (b) references to \"including\" or \"includes\" shall mean \"including, without limitation\" or \"includes, without limitation\"; (c) references to any agreement, document, or instrument mean such agreement, document, or instrument as amended, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time in accordance with its terms; (d) references to \"herein,\" \"hereof,\" \"hereunder,\" and similar terms refer to this Agreement as a whole, including all Exhibits, Schedules, and Statements of Work; (e) all references to Articles, Sections, Exhibits, Schedules, and Statements of Work refer to those of this Agreement; and (f) headings are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the interpretation or construction of any provision of this Agreement.

**Section 2.3 License Restrictions.** Licensee shall not, and shall not permit any third party to:

>

>
> \(g\) attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Licensed Software, any related systems or networks, or any accounts of other users;
>
> \(h\) transmit any viruses, malware, worms, trojans, or other harmful or malicious code through or to the Licensed Software;
>
> \(i\) interfere with or disrupt the integrity or performance of the Licensed Software or any data contained therein; or
>
> \(j\) benchmark, test, or evaluate the Licensed Software for the purpose of developing a competing product or service, or disclose any benchmark or performance test results to any third party without Licensor\'s prior written consent.

**Section 2.4 Reservation of Rights.** Except for the limited License expressly granted under this Article II, Licensor reserves all rights, title, and interest in and to the Licensed Software, the Documentation, and all Intellectual Property Rights therein. No rights are granted to Licensee hereunder by implication, estoppel, or otherwise, except as expressly set forth in this Agreement.

**Section 2.5 Authorized User Management.** Licensee shall be responsible for: (a) ensuring that all Authorized Users comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement, including the Acceptable Use Policy; (b) maintaining the confidentiality of all login credentials and access codes associated with its accounts; and (c) all activities that occur under its accounts. Licensee shall promptly notify Licensor of any unauthorized use of its accounts or any other breach of security.

**Section 2.6 Third-Party Components.** The Licensed Software may incorporate or be provided with certain third-party software components, libraries, or modules (\"**Third-Party Components**\"). Licensee\'s use of Third-Party Components shall be subject to the applicable third-party license terms, which are identified in the Documentation. Licensor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that all Third-Party Components included in the Licensed Software are properly licensed and do not infringe the Intellectual Property Rights of any third party.

**[ARTICLE III --- IMPLEMENTATION SERVICES]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 Statements of Work.** Licensor shall provide Implementation Services to Licensee as described in each Statement of Work executed by the Parties. The initial Statement of Work (\"**SOW-1**\") is attached hereto as Schedule 1 and is incorporated by reference. Additional Statements of Work may be executed by the Parties from time to time and, upon execution, shall be incorporated into and governed by this Agreement.

**Section 3.2 Performance of Implementation Services.** Licensor shall perform the Implementation Services in a professional and workmanlike manner, consistent with generally accepted industry standards and practices for enterprise software implementations in the healthcare sector. Licensor shall assign qualified personnel with appropriate skills and experience to perform the Implementation Services.

**Section 3.3 Licensor Personnel.** Licensor shall designate a project manager (the \"**Licensor Project Manager**\") who shall serve as Licensee\'s primary point of contact for matters relating to the Implementation Services. Licensor shall not remove or replace the Licensor Project Manager without providing Licensee at least fifteen (15) Business Days\' prior written notice, except in cases of resignation, termination of employment, illness, or other circumstances beyond Licensor\'s reasonable control. In the event of such removal or replacement, Licensor shall promptly designate a replacement project manager of comparable qualifications.

**Section 3.4 Licensee Responsibilities.** Licensee acknowledges that the timely and successful completion of the Implementation Services depends upon Licensee\'s fulfillment of certain obligations and responsibilities, including those set forth in Section 4 of each applicable Statement of Work (collectively, \"**Licensee Responsibilities**\"). Without limiting the foregoing, Licensee shall:

> \(a\) designate a project manager (the \"**Licensee Project Manager**\") who shall have sufficient authority to make decisions on behalf of Licensee relating to the Implementation Services, and who shall serve as Licensor\'s primary point of contact. The Licensee Project Manager shall be available to participate in regularly scheduled project meetings and shall respond to Licensor inquiries within two (2) Business Days;
>
> \(b\) provide Licensor with timely access to Licensee\'s facilities, systems, networks, data, and personnel as reasonably required for Licensor to perform the Implementation Services;
>
> \(c\) make available qualified subject matter experts, business analysts, and technical personnel as reasonably requested by Licensor for requirements gathering, configuration, testing, and training activities;
>
> \(d\) provide complete and accurate information, data, specifications, and materials as required under the applicable Statement of Work, within the timeframes specified therein;
>
> \(e\) perform all data migration activities, including the extraction, transformation, and loading of legacy data into the Licensed Software, using qualified personnel or a third-party systems integrator retained by Licensee at Licensee\'s sole cost and expense;
>
> \(f\) perform Acceptance Testing in accordance with Section 5.3; and
>
> \(g\) comply with all other Licensee Responsibilities specified in the applicable Statement of Work.

**Section 3.5 Schedule Adjustments.** If Licensee\'s failure to timely fulfill any Licensee Responsibility causes a delay in Licensor\'s performance of the Implementation Services, the applicable project schedule and milestones shall be adjusted on a day-for-day basis to account for such delay. Licensor shall promptly notify Licensee in writing of any such delay and the expected impact on the project schedule. Licensor shall not be liable for any failure to meet the Target Go-Live Date or any other milestone to the extent such failure is caused by Licensee\'s breach of its obligations under this Section 3.4 or the applicable Statement of Work.

**Section 3.6 Project Governance.** The Parties shall establish a project governance structure as set forth in the applicable Statement of Work, which shall include: (a) a steering committee composed of senior representatives of each Party, which shall meet no less frequently than monthly to review project status, resolve escalated issues, and approve material changes; (b) weekly project status meetings between the respective Project Managers and implementation team leads; and (c) written project status reports to be prepared by Licensor on a bi-weekly basis.

**Section 3.7 Site Access and Cooperation.** Licensee shall provide Licensor\'s personnel with reasonable access to Licensee\'s facilities and systems during normal business hours, and at other times as reasonably agreed upon by the Parties, to the extent necessary for Licensor to perform the Implementation Services. All Licensor personnel accessing Licensee\'s facilities shall comply with Licensee\'s reasonable security, confidentiality, and conduct policies, as communicated to Licensor in writing.

**Section 3.8 Subcontractors.** Licensor may engage subcontractors to perform portions of the Implementation Services, provided that: (a) Licensor shall remain responsible for the performance of all Implementation Services regardless of whether performed by a subcontractor; (b) Licensor shall ensure that all subcontractors are bound by obligations of confidentiality and data protection no less restrictive than those set forth in this Agreement; and (c) for subcontractors who will have access to Protected Health Information or other Personal Data, Licensor shall ensure compliance with all applicable HIPAA requirements, including the execution of appropriate business associate agreements.

**[ARTICLE IV --- MAINTENANCE AND SUPPORT]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 Maintenance and Support Services.** During the License Term, and subject to Licensee\'s timely payment of the applicable Maintenance and Support Fees, Licensor shall provide Maintenance and Support Services in accordance with the Service Level Agreement attached as Exhibit B. Maintenance and Support Services shall include:

> \(a\) **Error Correction.** Licensor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to correct Material Nonconformities reported by Licensee in accordance with the severity classifications and response time commitments set forth in the Service Level Agreement.
>
> \(b\) **Updates and Patches.** Licensor shall provide Licensee with all updates, patches, bug fixes, and error corrections that Licensor makes generally available to its licensees of the Licensed Software at no additional charge.
>
> \(c\) **Upgrades.** Licensor shall provide Licensee with access to new versions and major releases of the Licensed Software that Licensor makes generally available to its licensees of the Licensed Software during the License Term, subject to the upgrade schedule and procedures set forth in the Service Level Agreement.
>
> \(d\) **Help Desk Support.** Licensor shall provide Licensee with access to Licensor\'s technical support help desk during the hours specified in the Service Level Agreement, via telephone, email, and online ticketing system.
>
> \(e\) **Knowledge Base.** Licensor shall provide Licensee with access to Licensor\'s online knowledge base, which includes FAQs, troubleshooting guides, best practices documentation, and technical articles.

**Section 4.2 Exclusions.** Licensor\'s obligations under this Article IV shall not extend to errors, defects, or issues arising from or relating to:

> \(a\) use of the Licensed Software in a manner not in accordance with the Documentation or the Acceptable Use Policy;
>
> \(b\) modifications to the Licensed Software made by anyone other than Licensor or its authorized representatives;
>
> \(c\) Licensee\'s failure to implement updates, patches, or upgrades made available by Licensor within a reasonable timeframe following their release;
>
> \(d\) hardware, software, network, or other infrastructure components not provided or specified by Licensor;
>
> \(e\) data quality issues, including errors in data migrated into the Licensed Software by Licensee or its third-party service providers;
>
> \(f\) third-party software, systems, or services with which the Licensed Software interfaces, to the extent the error or defect originates in such third-party software, systems, or services; or
>
> \(g\) Force Majeure Events.

**Section 4.3 Service Level Credits.** In the event that Licensor fails to meet the uptime or performance commitments set forth in the Service Level Agreement, Licensee shall be entitled to service level credits as described in Exhibit B. Service level credits shall be Licensee\'s sole and exclusive remedy for Licensor\'s failure to meet such service level commitments.

**Section 4.4 Licensee Support Obligations.** Licensee shall: (a) designate a primary support contact and a secondary support contact who shall serve as the interface between Licensee and Licensor\'s support team; (b) provide Licensor with sufficient information to diagnose and reproduce reported errors, including log files, screen captures, and step-by-step descriptions of the issue; (c) implement all updates, patches, and upgrades provided by Licensor in a timely manner; and (d) use commercially reasonable efforts to resolve issues arising from Licensee\'s own systems, networks, and data before escalating to Licensor.

**Section 4.5 Maintenance Windows.** Licensor shall be entitled to perform scheduled maintenance on the Licensed Software during the maintenance windows specified in the Service Level Agreement. Licensor shall provide Licensee with at least seventy-two (72) hours\' prior notice of scheduled maintenance that may result in downtime. Emergency maintenance may be performed without prior notice, provided that Licensor shall notify Licensee as soon as practicable before or after such emergency maintenance.

**[ARTICLE V --- ACCEPTANCE, TESTING, AND CHANGE MANAGEMENT]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 Deployment.** Licensor shall deploy the Licensed Software and Deliverables in accordance with the timeline and milestones set forth in the applicable Statement of Work. Upon deployment of each module or Deliverable, Licensor shall notify Licensee in writing that such module or Deliverable is available for Acceptance Testing (each, a \"**Deployment Notice**\").

**Section 5.2 Test Environment.** Prior to deployment into Licensee\'s production environment, Licensor shall make the Licensed Software available in a designated test or staging environment for purposes of Acceptance Testing. Licensee shall be responsible for providing adequate test data, test scripts, and test resources for Acceptance Testing.

**Section 5.3 Acceptance Testing.**

> \(a\) **Acceptance Testing Period.** Following Licensor\'s delivery of a Deployment Notice with respect to each module or Deliverable, Licensee shall have thirty (30) calendar days (the \"**Acceptance Testing Period**\") to conduct Acceptance Testing to determine whether such module or Deliverable performs substantially in accordance with the applicable specifications set forth in the Documentation and the applicable Statement of Work.
>
> \(b\) **Acceptance Criteria.** The acceptance criteria for each module or Deliverable shall be as set forth in the applicable Statement of Work. In the absence of specific acceptance criteria in the Statement of Work, the acceptance criteria shall be whether the module or Deliverable performs substantially in accordance with the Documentation.
>
> \(c\) **Notice of Material Nonconformity.** If Licensee determines during the Acceptance Testing Period that the module or Deliverable contains a Material Nonconformity, Licensee shall deliver written notice to Licensor (a \"**Nonconformity Notice**\") prior to the expiration of the Acceptance Testing Period. Each Nonconformity Notice shall describe in reasonable detail the nature of the Material Nonconformity and the specific acceptance criteria that have not been met.
>
> \(d\) **Deemed Acceptance.** If Licensee fails to deliver a Nonconformity Notice to Licensor within the Acceptance Testing Period, the applicable module or Deliverable shall be deemed accepted by Licensee (\"**Acceptance**\"). For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee\'s use of any module or Deliverable in its production environment for business purposes (other than Acceptance Testing) during the Acceptance Testing Period shall constitute Acceptance of such module or Deliverable.
>
> \(e\) **Cure Period.** Upon receipt of a Nonconformity Notice, Licensor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to correct the Material Nonconformity within thirty (30) calendar days (the \"**Cure Period**\"). Upon completion of the correction, Licensor shall deliver a revised Deployment Notice, and Licensee shall have an additional fifteen (15) calendar day period to re-test the corrected module or Deliverable. If Licensee does not deliver a subsequent Nonconformity Notice within such fifteen (15) calendar day period, the module or Deliverable shall be deemed accepted.
>
> \(f\) **Failure to Cure.** If Licensor is unable to correct a Material Nonconformity within two (2) successive Cure Periods (totaling sixty (60) calendar days), Licensee\'s sole and exclusive remedy shall be to: (i) accept the module or Deliverable with the nonconformity and receive a mutually agreed-upon credit against future Fees; or (ii) reject the module or Deliverable and receive a pro-rata refund of any pre-paid Fees attributable to such module or Deliverable, calculated from the date of rejection through the end of the then-current License Term.
>

**[EXHIBIT A --- SOFTWARE DESCRIPTION AND SPECIFICATIONS]{.underline}**

**A.1 Licensed Software.** The Licensed Software licensed under this Agreement is the **NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module**, a component of Licensor\'s NexusCore cloud-based enterprise resource planning and data analytics platform. The Healthcare Analytics Module provides the following core functional areas:

> \(a\) **Clinical Analytics.** Real-time and retrospective analysis of clinical data, including patient outcomes, treatment patterns, readmission rates, length of stay metrics, and clinical quality measures. Supports integration with electronic health record (\"EHR\") systems through Licensor\'s standard API framework.
>
> \(b\) **Financial Analytics.** Revenue cycle analytics, cost analysis, payer mix analysis, denials management analytics, and financial forecasting capabilities. Supports integration with hospital billing systems and practice management platforms.
>
> \(c\) **Operational Analytics.** Operational performance dashboards, capacity utilization analysis, staffing analytics, supply chain analytics, and workflow optimization tools.
>
> \(d\) **Population Health Analytics.** Population health management analytics, including risk stratification, care gap identification, chronic disease management analytics, and preventive care tracking.
>
> \(e\) **Regulatory Compliance Analytics.** Analytics and reporting tools designed to support compliance with healthcare regulatory requirements, including CMS quality reporting programs, HEDIS measures, and state-specific reporting requirements.
>
> \(f\) **Reporting and Visualization.** Configurable dashboards, ad-hoc reporting capabilities, scheduled report generation, data visualization tools, and export capabilities in multiple formats (PDF, Excel, CSV, HL7 FHIR).

**A.2 System Architecture.** The Licensed Software operates as a multi-tenant, cloud-hosted software-as-a-service application. The Licensed Software is hosted on enterprise-grade cloud infrastructure managed by Licensor or its designated hosting provider. Key architectural features include:

> \(a\) multi-tenant architecture with logical data separation;
>
> \(b\) role-based access control and authentication (including SAML 2.0 and OAuth 2.0 support);
>
> \(c\) encryption of data in transit (TLS 1.2 or higher) and at rest (AES-256);
>
> \(d\) high-availability architecture with geographic redundancy;
>
> \(e\) RESTful API framework for system integration;
>
> \(f\) configurable ETL (extract, transform, load) pipelines for data ingestion; and
>
> \(g\) scalable processing engine for real-time and batch analytics.

**A.3 Supported Integrations.** The Licensed Software is designed to integrate with major EHR platforms through Licensor\'s standard API framework, subject to proper configuration and the provision of accurate integration specifications by Licensee. Licensor maintains pre-built connectors for the following EHR platforms as of the Effective Date: Epic, Cerner (Oracle Health), MEDITECH, Allscripts, athenahealth, and eClinicalWorks. Integration with other EHR platforms may require custom API development pursuant to a Change Order.

**A.4 Documentation.** Licensor shall provide the following Documentation in connection with the Licensed Software:

> \(a\) Administrator Guide;
>
> \(b\) End-User Guide;
>
> \(c\) API Integration Guide and Technical Reference;
>
> \(d\) Data Dictionary and Schema Documentation;
>
> \(e\) Configuration Guide;
>
> \(f\) Release Notes (for each update, patch, and upgrade); and
>
> \(g\) Online Help System accessible within the Licensed Software.

**A.5 Technical Requirements.** Licensee is responsible for ensuring that its end-user systems and network environment meet the minimum technical requirements specified in the Documentation, including: (a) supported web browsers and versions; (b) minimum internet bandwidth requirements; and (c) network configuration requirements for API integrations. Licensor shall not be responsible for performance issues arising from Licensee\'s failure to meet such technical requirements.

**[EXHIBIT B --- SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**B.1 Uptime Commitment.** Licensor commits to a monthly uptime percentage of ninety-nine and seven-tenths percent (99.7%) for the Licensed Software, measured on a calendar-month basis, excluding Scheduled Maintenance Windows and Force Majeure Events (the \"**Uptime Commitment**\"). \"**Uptime Percentage**\" is calculated as: ((Total Minutes in Calendar Month -- Downtime Minutes) / Total Minutes in Calendar Month) x 100.

**B.2 Scheduled Maintenance Windows.** Licensor\'s standard Scheduled Maintenance Window is Sunday, 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM Central Time. Licensor shall provide at least seventy-two (72) hours\' prior notice of Scheduled Maintenance that may result in downtime exceeding thirty (30) minutes. Total Scheduled Maintenance downtime shall not exceed eight (8) hours per calendar month.

**B.3 Severity Classifications and Response Times.**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Severity Level**        **Description**                                                                      **Initial Response Time**   **Target Resolution Time**
  ------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --------------------------- ----------------------------
  Severity 1 --- Critical   Production system down or major functionality unavailable; no workaround available   1 hour (24/7)               4 hours

  Severity 2 --- High       Major functionality impaired; workaround available but degraded performance          4 hours (business hours)    1 Business Day

  Severity 3 --- Medium     Minor functionality impaired; workaround available with minimal impact               8 business hours            5 Business Days

  Severity 4 --- Low        Cosmetic issue, documentation error, or enhancement request                          2 Business Days             Next scheduled release
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**B.4 Escalation Procedures.**

> \(a\) **Level 1 --- Help Desk.** Initial support contact via telephone, email, or online ticketing system. Available Monday through Friday, 7:00 AM to 9:00 PM Central Time, and Saturday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Central Time (excluding Licensor-observed holidays).
>
> \(b\) **Level 2 --- Technical Support Engineers.** Escalation for issues not resolved at Level 1 within the applicable response time. Available during business hours.
>
> \(c\) **Level 3 --- Engineering Team.** Escalation for complex issues requiring development-level investigation or code changes. Coordinated by Licensor\'s assigned Technical Account Manager.
>
> \(d\) **Level 4 --- Executive Escalation.** For Severity 1 issues not resolved within the target resolution time, escalation to Licensor\'s VP of Customer Success and, if necessary, to the Licensor Project Manager and Steering Committee.

**B.5 Service Level Credits.**

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Monthly Uptime Percentage**       **Service Level Credit (% of Monthly Maintenance & Support Fee)**
  ----------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------
  99.0% to \< 99.7%                   5%

  98.0% to \< 99.0%                   10%

  95.0% to \< 98.0%                   20%

  Below 95.0%                         30%
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Service level credits shall be applied as a credit against Licensee\'s next invoice. In no event shall service level credits in any calendar month exceed thirty percent (30%) of the Monthly Maintenance and Support Fee for such month. Service level credits are Licensee\'s sole and exclusive remedy for Licensor\'s failure to meet the Uptime Commitment.

**B.6 Reporting.** Licensor shall provide Licensee with monthly service level reports within ten (10) Business Days following the end of each calendar month, summarizing uptime performance, support ticket statistics, and any service level credit calculations.

**[EXHIBIT C --- DATA MIGRATION ADVISORY SERVICES]{.underline}**

**C.1 Scope of Advisory Services.** Licensor shall provide Data Migration Advisory Services to assist Licensee in planning and executing the migration of Licensee\'s legacy data from HealthBridge 4.0 (and any other designated source systems) into the Licensed Software. Data Migration Advisory Services are advisory in nature and include:

> \(a\) **Data Assessment.** Licensor shall review and assess Licensee\'s existing data sources, data structures, data quality, and data volumes to develop migration recommendations.
>
> \(b\) **Migration Planning.** Licensor shall assist Licensee in developing a data migration plan, including identification of data mapping requirements, transformation rules, data cleansing recommendations, and migration sequencing.
>
> \(c\) **Technical Guidance.** Licensor shall provide technical guidance on the use of the Licensed Software\'s data ingestion interfaces, including the ETL pipeline configuration, data format requirements, and API-based data loading procedures.
>
> \(d\) **Validation Framework.** Licensor shall provide a data validation framework and recommended test scripts that Licensee may use to verify the accuracy and completeness of migrated data.
>
> \(e\) **Knowledge Transfer.** Licensor shall conduct knowledge transfer sessions with Licensee\'s technical team and/or Licensee\'s designated systems integrator regarding data migration best practices, common pitfalls, and recommended approaches.

**C.2 Licensee Responsibility for Data Migration.** Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, **Licensee shall be solely responsible for all data migration activities**, including the extraction, transformation, loading, validation, and reconciliation of legacy data into the Licensed Software. Licensor\'s Data Migration Advisory Services are provided on a consultative basis and do not constitute the performance of data migration by Licensor. Licensor shall have no responsibility or liability for:

> \(a\) the accuracy, completeness, integrity, or quality of data migrated into the Licensed Software by Licensee or its third-party service providers;
>
> \(b\) data loss, corruption, or errors occurring during the migration process;
>
> \(c\) the selection, engagement, management, or performance of any third-party systems integrator or other service provider retained by Licensee to perform data migration activities;
>
> \(d\) delays in the project schedule attributable to data migration issues; or
>
> \(e\) defects in the Licensed Software\'s performance to the extent caused by inaccurate, incomplete, or corrupted migrated data.

**C.3 Third-Party Systems Integrator.** Licensee acknowledges and agrees that it intends to retain a third-party systems integrator to perform data migration activities. Licensor shall cooperate with Licensee\'s designated systems integrator as reasonably necessary to facilitate data migration, including providing reasonable access to the Licensed Software\'s data ingestion interfaces and responding to reasonable technical inquiries. Licensor shall not be deemed to have any contractual, employment, agency, or other relationship with Licensee\'s systems integrator, and Licensee shall be solely responsible for the acts and omissions of its systems integrator.

**C.4 Fees.** The Fees for Data Migration Advisory Services are One Million Six Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$1,600,000), payable as follows:

> \(a\) \$400,000 upon execution of this Agreement (data assessment and initial planning);
>
> \(b\) \$400,000 upon delivery of the data migration plan and technical specifications;
>
> \(c\) \$400,000 upon completion of knowledge transfer sessions and delivery of the validation framework; and
>
> \(d\) \$400,000 upon Go-Live.

**Section 1 --- Scope of Implementation Services**

**1.1 Overview.** Licensor shall provide Implementation Services for the deployment of the NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module within Licensee\'s IT environment, in accordance with the terms and conditions of the Agreement and this SOW-1. The Implementation Services shall encompass the following workstreams:

> \(a\) **System Configuration.** Configuration of the Healthcare Analytics Module to align with Licensee\'s organizational structure, workflows, reporting requirements, and business rules, including configuration of user roles, permissions, data hierarchies, and analytics parameters.
>
> \(b\) **User Interface Customization.** Customization of the Licensed Software\'s user interface, including branded dashboards, custom navigation layouts, and role-specific views tailored to Licensee\'s clinical, financial, and operational user groups.
>
> \(c\) **Report Template Development.** Development of up to fifty (50) custom report templates based on Licensee\'s reporting requirements, including clinical quality reports, financial performance reports, operational dashboards, and regulatory compliance reports.
>
> \(d\) **API Integration.** Development and testing of API connectors to integrate the Licensed Software with Licensee\'s existing electronic health record (\"EHR\") systems and other designated source systems. Licensor shall be responsible for building the API connectors in accordance with the integration specifications provided by Licensee. Licensee shall be responsible for providing complete and accurate API specifications, test environments, and test data within the timeframes specified in the Milestone Schedule (Section 6).
>
> \(e\) **Training.** Delivery of administrator training and end-user training as described in Section 5 of this SOW-1.

**1.2 Exclusions.** The following activities are expressly excluded from the scope of Implementation Services under this SOW-1:

> \(a\) data migration (including extraction, transformation, and loading of legacy data) --- see Section 3.2;
>
> \(b\) development of custom analytics models or algorithms not described in the Documentation;
>
> \(c\) hardware procurement, installation, or configuration;
>
> \(d\) network design, engineering, or optimization;
>
> \(e\) integration with systems not identified in the integration specifications provided by Licensee pursuant to Section 1.1(d); and
>
> \(f\) any other work not expressly described in this SOW-1, unless added through a mutually executed Change Order.

  Integration Engineer(s)             API connector development and testing

  Training Lead                       Development and delivery of training curriculum

  Quality Assurance Lead              Testing coordination and quality assurance
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**2.2** Licensor shall: (a) perform the Implementation Services in accordance with the Milestone Schedule; (b) provide Licensee with bi-weekly project status reports; (c) participate in weekly project status meetings; (d) promptly notify Licensee of any risks, issues, or dependencies that may impact the project schedule; and (e) comply with Licensee\'s reasonable security, confidentiality, and facility access policies.

**Section 3 --- Licensee Responsibilities**

**3.1 General.** Licensee acknowledges that the timely and successful completion of the Implementation Services depends upon Licensee\'s performance of the Licensee Responsibilities set forth in this Section 3. Delays or failures by Licensee in performing the Licensee Responsibilities may result in schedule adjustments in accordance with Section 3.5 of the Agreement.

**3.2 Data Migration.** Licensee shall be solely responsible for all data migration activities, including extraction, transformation, and loading of legacy data into NexusCore. Licensor shall provide advisory consulting services as described in Exhibit C of the Agreement, but shall have no responsibility for the accuracy, completeness, or integrity of migrated data. Licensee shall retain a qualified systems integrator, at Licensee\'s sole cost and expense, to perform data migration activities. Licensee\'s designated systems integrator shall coordinate with Licensor\'s project team as necessary to ensure compatibility of migrated data with the Licensed Software\'s data models and ingestion interfaces.

**3.3 Licensee Project Manager.** Licensee shall designate a project manager (the \"**Licensee Project Manager**\") within five (5) Business Days of the SOW-1 Effective Date. The Licensee Project Manager shall: (a) have sufficient authority to make day-to-day project decisions on behalf of Licensee; (b) be available to participate in all scheduled project meetings; (c) respond to Licensor inquiries within two (2) Business Days; (d) coordinate the activities of Licensee\'s internal team and any third-party service providers retained by Licensee; and (e) serve as the escalation point for project issues requiring Licensee action. In the event that the Licensee Project Manager is no longer available (due to resignation, reassignment, or any other reason), Licensee shall designate a replacement of comparable qualifications within ten (10) Business Days.

**3.4 Access and Information.** Licensee shall:

> \(a\) provide Licensor with timely access to Licensee\'s facilities, systems, networks, and data as reasonably required for Licensor to perform the Implementation Services;
>
> \(b\) provide complete and accurate API integration specifications, including endpoint documentation, authentication protocols, data format requirements, and test credentials, within the timeframes specified in the Milestone Schedule;
>
> \(c\) make available qualified subject matter experts, business analysts, clinical informaticists, and technical personnel as reasonably requested by Licensor for requirements gathering, configuration validation, and user acceptance testing;
>
> \(d\) provide a designated test environment with representative production data for integration testing and Acceptance Testing; and
>
> \(e\) perform Acceptance Testing in accordance with Section 5.3 of the Agreement.

**Section 4 --- Acceptance Criteria**

**4.1 Module Acceptance Criteria.** The following acceptance criteria shall apply to the Healthcare Analytics Module deployed under this SOW-1:

> \(a\) the Licensed Software has been deployed into Licensee\'s production environment and is accessible by Authorized Users;
>
> \(b\) all system configurations specified in the approved requirements document have been completed and verified;
>
> \(c\) all custom report templates described in the approved report specification document have been delivered and function as specified;
>
> \(d\) all API integrations described in the approved integration specification document have been tested and demonstrated to exchange data accurately between the Licensed Software and Licensee\'s designated source systems in the test environment;
>
> \(e\) administrator and end-user training has been delivered in accordance with the training plan; and
>
> \(f\) no Severity 1 (Critical) or Severity 2 (High) defects remain unresolved at the time of Go-Live, as documented in the defect tracking system maintained by Licensor.

**4.2 Acceptance Testing.** Acceptance Testing shall be conducted in accordance with Section 5.3 of the Agreement. The Acceptance Testing Period for the Healthcare Analytics Module shall be thirty (30) calendar days following the Go-Live Date.

**Section 5 --- Training**

**5.1 Training Plan.** Licensor shall develop a training plan and training curriculum within thirty (30) days following the completion of the requirements gathering phase. The training plan shall include: (a) identification of training audiences (administrators, power users, and end users); (b) training objectives and learning outcomes; (c) training modalities (instructor-led, web-based, self-paced); (d) training schedule; and (e) training materials.

**5.2 Administrator Training.** Licensor shall provide up to forty (40) hours of instructor-led administrator training, covering system administration, configuration management, security administration, report administration, and integration management. Administrator training shall be delivered to Licensee\'s designated system administrators prior to Go-Live.

**5.3 End-User Training.** Licensor shall provide up to eighty (80) hours of instructor-led end-user training, delivered in role-based sessions tailored to the needs of Licensee\'s clinical, financial, and operational user groups. End-user training shall be delivered in the period commencing two (2) weeks prior to Go-Live and concluding four (4) weeks following Go-Live.

**5.4 Training Materials.** Licensor shall provide training materials, including instructor guides, participant workbooks, quick-reference guides, and recorded training videos. Training materials shall become the property of Licensee and may be used by Licensee for internal training purposes following the conclusion of Licensor-delivered training.

**Section 6 --- Milestone Schedule**

**Note:** The API Integration Specifications (M3) are due from Licensee on May 6, 2022, which is fifty-two (52) days following the SOW-1 Effective Date. Licensee\'s failure to timely deliver the integration specifications shall result in a corresponding day-for-day adjustment to subsequent milestones in accordance with Section 3.5 of the Agreement.

**Section 7 --- Fees**

The total Fees for Implementation Services under this SOW-1 are Three Million Eight Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$3,800,000), payable in accordance with the Implementation Services Fee Payment Schedule set forth in Exhibit D, Section D.3 of the Agreement.

**Section 8 --- Assumptions**

This SOW-1 is based on the following assumptions. If any assumption proves to be inaccurate, the Parties shall discuss the impact on scope, schedule, and Fees, and shall address any changes through the Change Order process described in Section 5.4 of the Agreement.

> \(i\) English is the only language required for the Licensed Software user interface, reports, and training materials.

result:
**STONEBRIDGE & CALLOWAY LLP** 600 Congress Avenue, Suite 2800 Austin, TX 78701

**PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL** **ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT**

**MEMORANDUM**

**TO:** Margaret \"Meg\" Calloway, Lead Partner David Arsenault, Senior Associate

**FROM:** Litigation Research Group, Stonebridge & Calloway LLP

**DATE:** December 18, 2023

**RE:** Case Law Research Compilation --- *Arcadia Health Systems, LLC v. Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.*, No. 1:23-cv-00847-CMA (W.D. Tex., Austin Division)

**[I. PURPOSE AND SCOPE]{.underline}**

This memorandum compiles and analyzes the principal authorities relevant to Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.\'s anticipated Rule 12(b)(6) motion to dismiss the First Amended Complaint (\"FAC\") filed by Arcadia Health Systems, LLC. The research is organized by substantive issue and includes case summaries, key holdings, and notes on the applicability of each authority to the facts of our case. Where choice-of-law considerations affect the analysis, we note the governing jurisdiction. The MSLSA\'s Section 12.7 designates Delaware law for contractual claims, while Texas law likely governs the tort and statutory claims under the most-significant-relationship test.

The motion filing deadline is **January 15, 2024**. This memorandum is intended to serve as the research foundation for the brief and proposed order.

**[II. PLEADING STANDARDS UNDER RULE 12(b)(6)]{.underline}**

**A. \*Bell Atlantic Corp. v. Twombly\*, 550 U.S. 544 (2007)**

**Court:** United States Supreme Court

**Factual Background:** Subscribers to local telephone service brought a class action antitrust suit against incumbent local exchange carriers (\"ILECs\"), alleging that the ILECs had conspired to restrain trade in violation of Section 1 of the Sherman Act. Plaintiffs alleged parallel conduct among the ILECs --- specifically, that each ILEC refrained from entering competitors\' territories and engaged in similar behavior to inhibit the growth of competitive local exchange carriers (\"CLECs\"). The district court dismissed for failure to state a claim. The Second Circuit reversed. The Supreme Court granted certiorari.

**Holding:** The Supreme Court reversed the Second Circuit, holding that a complaint must plead \"enough facts to state a claim to relief that is plausible on its face.\" *Id.* at 570. The Court retired the \"no set of facts\" standard from *Conley v. Gibson*, 355 U.S. 41 (1957), holding that it was \"best forgotten as an incomplete, negative gloss on an accepted pleading standard.\" *Id.* at 563. A plaintiff must allege facts that \"raise a right to relief above the speculative level.\" *Id.* at 555. Mere \"labels and conclusions\" or \"a formulaic recitation of the elements of a cause of action\" are insufficient. *Id.* at 555. The Court emphasized that the plausibility standard requires \"more than a sheer possibility that a defendant has acted unlawfully.\" *Id.* at 556.

**Key Analytical Framework:**

1\. The court must accept all well-pleaded factual allegations as true.

2\. Legal conclusions couched as factual allegations are not entitled to the presumption of truth.

3\. The court must determine whether the factual allegations, taken as true, plausibly give rise to an entitlement to relief.

4\. Plausibility is \"not akin to a \'probability requirement,\' but it asks for more than a sheer possibility.\" *Id.* at 556.

**Relevance to Our Case:** *Twombly* provides the foundational standard for evaluating all five counts of the FAC. Arcadia\'s allegations of software \"defects\" and implementation \"failures\" must rise above conclusory assertions to state plausible claims. Where Arcadia relies on characterizations unsupported by specific factual allegations --- particularly regarding the nature and extent of alleged software nonconformities --- the *Twombly* framework supports dismissal. The FAC\'s sweeping allegation that Meridian \"delivered defective software\" is precisely the type of conclusory label that *Twombly* holds insufficient.

**B. \*Ashcroft v. Iqbal\*, 556 U.S. 662 (2009)**

**Court:** United States Supreme Court

**Factual Background:** A Pakistani Muslim detained following the September 11 attacks brought a *Bivens* action against former Attorney General John Ashcroft and FBI Director Robert Mueller, alleging that the officials adopted an unconstitutional policy of detaining Arab Muslim men based on race, religion, and national origin. The district court denied the defendants\' motion to dismiss. The Second Circuit affirmed.

**Holding:** The Supreme Court reversed, refining and extending the *Twombly* plausibility standard to all civil actions. The Court articulated a two-pronged approach: first, courts must identify the allegations in the complaint that are not entitled to the assumption of truth because they are conclusory; second, courts must consider the remaining well-pleaded factual allegations, assume their veracity, and then determine whether they plausibly suggest an entitlement to relief. *Id.* at 679. The Court reiterated that \"\[t\]hreadbare recitals of the elements of a cause of action, supported by mere conclusory statements, do not suffice.\" *Id.* at 678.

**Key Principles:**

1\. The plausibility standard of *Twombly* is \"not akin to a probability requirement\" but \"calls for enough fact to raise a reasonable expectation that discovery will reveal evidence\" supporting the claim. *Id.* at 678 (quoting *Twombly*).

2\. \"Where a complaint pleads facts that are \'merely consistent with\' a defendant\'s liability, it \'stops short of the line between possibility and plausibility of entitlement to relief.\'\" *Id.* at 678.

3\. Determining plausibility is \"a context-specific task that requires the reviewing court to draw on its judicial experience and common sense.\" *Id.* at 679.

**Relevance to Our Case:** *Iqbal*\'s two-pronged approach is directly applicable. The FAC contains numerous conclusory assertions --- *e.g.*, that Meridian \"knew or should have known\" its software would not perform as described, that Meridian \"intentionally concealed\" deficiencies, and that Meridian\'s pre-sale representations were \"materially false.\" Under *Iqbal*, the Court must strip away these conclusory assertions and evaluate whether the remaining factual allegations plausibly state a claim. Critically, *Iqbal* instructs courts to consider whether an \"obvious alternative explanation\" exists for the alleged conduct. *Id.* at 682. Here, the 137-day Go-Live delay and alleged performance issues have obvious alternative explanations rooted in Arcadia\'s own conduct --- the 45-day project manager gap, the 75-day delay in providing API specifications, and Linden Park Consulting\'s data migration errors --- which undermine the plausibility of Arcadia\'s conclusory assertion that Meridian is solely responsible.

**C. \*Lormand v. US Unwired, Inc.\*, 565 F.3d 228 (5th Cir. 2009)**

**Court:** United States Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit

**Factual Background:** Shareholders brought securities fraud claims against US Unwired, a wireless telecommunications company, alleging material misrepresentations and omissions regarding the company\'s subscriber growth and churn rates. The district court dismissed certain claims for failure to state a claim.

**Holding:** The Fifth Circuit, applying *Twombly* and *Iqbal*, held that to survive a motion to dismiss, a plaintiff must plead \"specific facts, not mere conclusory allegations.\" *Id.* at 255--56. The court emphasized that the plausibility standard requires a plaintiff to plead \"factual content that allows the court to draw the reasonable inference that the defendant is liable for the misconduct alleged.\" *Id.* at 257. The Fifth Circuit further clarified that in evaluating plausibility, a court should consider the complaint as a whole, including documents attached to or incorporated by reference into the complaint.

**Key Principles for Our Case:**

1\. In the Fifth Circuit, courts \"are not bound to accept as true a legal conclusion couched as a factual allegation.\" *Id.* at 255--56 (quoting *Iqbal*).

2\. A complaint that alleges facts \"merely consistent with\" liability does not meet the plausibility threshold. The plaintiff must nudge claims \"across the line from conceivable to plausible.\"

3\. Courts may consider documents that are \"referred to in the plaintiff\'s complaint and are central to the plaintiff\'s claim\" without converting the motion into one for summary judgment. This is critical here because the MSLSA, SOW-1, and Change Orders are central to Arcadia\'s claims and may be considered on the motion.

**Relevance to Our Case:** *Lormand* confirms that Judge Alvarez may consider the MSLSA, SOW-1, and the four Change Orders (CO-001 through CO-004) on the motion to dismiss, even if not physically attached to the FAC, because they are referenced in and central to Arcadia\'s claims. The contractual provisions --- particularly Section 5.3 (deemed acceptance), Section 8.1 (liability cap), Section 8.2 (consequential damages waiver), Section 9.1 (limited warranty), and Section 12.1 (integration clause) --- can and should be placed before the Court at the 12(b)(6) stage.

**[III. RULE 9(b) --- FRAUD PARTICULARITY REQUIREMENT]{.underline}**

**A. \*Benchmark Electronics, Inc. v. J.M. Huber Corp.\*, 343 F.3d 719 (5th Cir. 2003)**

**Court:** United States Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit

**Factual Background:** Benchmark Electronics, an electronics manufacturer, brought fraud and breach of contract claims against J.M. Huber, alleging that Huber made false representations about the quality and performance characteristics of a specialty chemical compound used in Benchmark\'s manufacturing processes. The district court dismissed the fraud claims for failure to plead with the particularity required by Rule 9(b).

**Holding:** The Fifth Circuit affirmed the dismissal, holding that Rule 9(b) requires a plaintiff alleging fraud to \"specify the statements contended to be fraudulent, identify the speaker, state when and where the statements were made, and explain why the statements were fraudulent.\" *Id.* at 724. The court described this as the \"who, what, when, where, and why\" of the alleged fraud. The court emphasized that Rule 9(b) serves three purposes: (1) ensuring that the defendant has \"fair notice\" of the claims against it; (2) protecting defendants from \"harm to their goodwill and reputation\"; and (3) reducing the number of \"strike suits.\" *Id.* at 724.

**Key Principles:**

1\. Rule 9(b) applies to \"all cases where the gravamen of the claim is fraud even though the theory supporting the claim is not technically termed fraud.\" *Id.* at 724.

2\. The particularity requirement extends to claims that \"sound in fraud\" --- which may encompass negligent misrepresentation and DTPA claims where the underlying theory is fraudulent or deceptive conduct.

3\. General allegations that a defendant \"made misrepresentations\" or \"knew\" statements were false are insufficient without specificity as to the precise statements, their context, and the basis for the allegation of falsity.

**Relevance to Our Case:** The FAC alleges that James Poletti made fraudulent misrepresentations during the October 12, 2021 and November 18, 2021 presentations. While the FAC identifies Poletti and the approximate dates, it fails to plead with specificity *why* the alleged statements were fraudulent at the time they were made. Arcadia does not allege that Meridian knew NexusCore could not integrate with EHR platforms --- merely that the integration allegedly did not perform as expected after Go-Live.
